skill.name: agentdb-advanced-features
skill.description: Guides advanced AgentDB use for QUIC synchronization, multi-database layouts, distance metrics, hybrid search, and production database operations.
skill.summary: Build distributed AgentDB systems with advanced search, sync, and database management patterns.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, database, and blocker patterns, but review shows they are in SKILL.md documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The skill should publish with a medium warning because it instructs users to run real CLI, SQLite, network synchronization, ping, node, and sudo firewall commands.

medium_findings.title: Operational Commands In Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill includes shell examples for npx agentdb, sqlite3, node, ping, and sudo ufw. These are documentation examples, not code executed by the skill, but users could change databases, run network services, or alter firewall rules if copied without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present inside fenced documentation blocks. They are not auto-executed, but the examples perform real system, network, and database operations if a user runs them.

medium_findings.title: Network Synchronization Configuration Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill teaches QUIC synchronization with peer addresses and ports. The addresses are private examples, but enabling distributed sync can expose data across nodes if users configure peers or firewalls incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network configuration is intentional and central to the skill. The risk is contextual configuration exposure, not evidence of malicious network behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Backtick Execution Matches
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings map to Markdown fenced code delimiters and command examples. No Ruby backtick execution or skill-controlled shell invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown examples rather than executable Ruby or JavaScript code. The only file is SKILL.md, with no scripts or package entry points.

low_findings.title: False Positive Blocker Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2 keyword, and reconnaissance blocker hits appear to match ordinary text such as performance claims, mathematical example code, and section headings. No prompt injection, malicious command-and-control instructions, credential access, or obfuscation were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal AgentDB documentation and sample TypeScript. I found no text attempting to override evaluator instructions or hide behavior.

low_findings.title: Example Local Database Paths
low_findings.description: The SQLite database findings refer to example file names and local AgentDB paths. They do not include secrets or bundled database files, but users should avoid running examples against production data without backups.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database references are literal documentation examples. The risk is user operational impact, not hidden sensitive data inside the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privilege-Changing Firewall Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The troubleshooting section includes sudo ufw allow for UDP port 4433. This is a legitimate QUIC troubleshooting command, but it changes host firewall policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact sudo firewall command is present. It is not malicious by itself, but it is privileged and security-sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database Import, Merge, Vacuum, And Reindex Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI section includes database export, import, merge, VACUUM, ANALYZE, and reindex commands. These are legitimate maintenance operations but can modify or overwrite local database state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly visible in the documentation. Their impact depends on user-selected database files.

content.value_statement: Advanced AgentDB deployments need clear patterns for sync, search, and database operations. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for distributed AgentDB design.
actual_capabilities: Explains AgentDB QUIC synchronization configuration with peers, ports, batching, retries, and compression.
Shows how to choose cosine, Euclidean, dot product, and custom distance approaches for vector search.
Demonstrates hybrid vector and metadata search patterns with filtering and result weighting.
Describes multi-database layouts, sharding by domain, connection pooling, and production error handling.
Provides CLI-oriented examples for import, export, merge, optimization, and troubleshooting workflows.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install AgentDB or validate a live deployment.
Examples require AgentDB, Node.js, and matching application code to be useful.
Network and firewall commands must be reviewed for each environment before use.
It does not provide complete security hardening for distributed database clusters.

use_cases.title: Design distributed memory sync
use_cases.description: Plan AgentDB peer synchronization across application nodes using QUIC settings and operational guardrails.

use_cases.title: Improve semantic retrieval
use_cases.description: Select distance metrics, hybrid filters, and MMR options for more relevant vector search results.

use_cases.title: Operate multiple AgentDB stores
use_cases.description: Organize domain databases, shards, backups, merges, and optimization tasks for production usage.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a distance metric
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for vector retrieval quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB advanced features skill to compare cosine, Euclidean, and dot product search for my embedding use case. Recommend one metric and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan hybrid search filters
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate search design with metadata constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB advanced features skill to design a hybrid search strategy for documents with metadata filters for date, category, author, and quality score.

prompt_templates.title: Design a multi-database layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB advanced features skill to propose a multi-database and sharding layout for knowledge, conversation, and code memories in a multi-agent system.

prompt_templates.title: Review QUIC sync readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB advanced features skill to review my planned QUIC synchronization deployment. Check peer configuration, ports, retry settings, monitoring, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: I need better retrieval from research documents with metadata filters.
output_examples.output: A recommended hybrid search plan that combines vector similarity with filters for year, category, citations, and result count.

output_examples.input: I want three AgentDB nodes to share memory across services.
output_examples.output: A deployment checklist covering QUIC enablement, peer addresses, sync interval, retry behavior, firewall review, and monitoring points.

output_examples.input: My AgentDB store has separate knowledge, conversation, and code memories.
output_examples.output: A database organization plan with domain separation, shard routing rules, backup considerations, and query routing guidance.

best_practices: Test database maintenance commands on backups before running them on important data.
Use private, authenticated network paths for QUIC peers and document every allowed port.
Benchmark distance metrics with representative embeddings before choosing production defaults.
anti_patterns: Do not copy sudo firewall commands into production without change control and approval.
Do not merge or import databases without a tested rollback plan.
Do not use one vector metric for every dataset without validating retrieval quality.

faq.question: Does this skill execute AgentDB commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples that a user may adapt and run manually.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains real shell, database, network, and sudo examples. They are useful but need review before execution.

faq.question: Does it cover hybrid vector search?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains metadata filters, result counts, weighting, and MMR-style result diversification.

faq.question: Does it include production operations guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers connection pooling, error handling, monitoring, database optimization, and troubleshooting.

faq.question: Can it secure a QUIC deployment by itself?
faq.answer: No. It gives configuration guidance, but teams must apply their own authentication, firewall, and network policies.
